answer:
The decay time follows an exponential distribution with median 80 years, so the mean is \(80 / \ln 2 \approx 115.5\) years.  
To specify the decay time to 3-digit accuracy means distinguishing about 1 part in 1000 of the mean, so the number of distinguishable intervals is roughly \(115.5 / (115.5/1000) = 1000\), requiring \(\log_2 1000 \approx 10\) bits.  
Thus the correct answer is C.

\boxed{C}
